title: 3 Legal Considerations for FX Drafts
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations for FX Drafts
3.1	Overview of Legal Considerations
This Chapter describes some legal considerations specific to FX Drafts. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for detailed AML information on requirements not found in this Chapter. Other federal and provincial legislation not covered in this Chapter can apply to FX Drafts (e.g., privacy legislation, human rights legislation) for which your financial institution must apply additional policies and procedures. Consult your financial institutionâs compliance officer or legal counsel for more information.
Related Operations Manual Program Parts
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
PCMLTFA Compliance Part
(BC)
PCMLTFA Compliance Part
(ON)
Note: 	The Operations Manual Program is available on a subscription basis. For more information about the Program, please contact Product Compliance & Design at
manuals@central1.com
or speak with your Central 1 Account Executive.
3.2	Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act and Regulations
The
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
apply to financial institutions and their agents (e.g., deposit-taking agents). Contact your financial institutionâs AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
3.3	Agreements for FX Drafts
To participate in FX Drafts, your financial institution must enter into an agreement with Central 1 and Convera. This agreement specifies that Convera is compliant with the
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
and processes payments in accordance with the laws and regulations of Canada, including but not limited to, those laws and regulations relating to anti-money laundering, anti-terrorism, foreign asset control, and economic and trade sanctions. Convera is also compliant with the Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) of the US Department of the Treasury regulations.
Under the agreement, financial institutions must immediately notify Convera if they identify suspicious activity involving Converaâs products and services. Financial institutions will share information with Convera to assist in meeting any regulatory obligation and/or responding to requests from correspondent banks.
END
